goal: Build a text-analysis app where a user can paste or write prose in an editor and view layered algorithmic highlights across many scoring systems, each mapped into five color bands derived from a mean-centered distribution.
constraints: The editor must update realtime highlight families in under 120 ms for passages up to 1,500 words on a modern laptop.
The UI must separate analyses into realtime, short-wait, and long-wait tiers so users understand latency before toggling an algorithm.
When multiple enabled algorithms overlap on the same character range, the renderer must show only the lowest severity band among the overlapping active highlights.
The controls panel must remain minimalist and predominantly black-and-white, with color reserved for highlight chips, swatches, and legend cues.
The first shipped version must run locally in a browser-based app without requiring user accounts.
must_have_features: Rich text input area with paste support and direct writing inside the editor.
Five-band highlight system per algorithm using one mean band and four standard-deviation-derived bands around it.
Right-side control system with grouped toggle switches by algorithm family and latency tier.
Conflict-resolution engine that computes visible highlight precedence when enabled algorithms overlap.
Realtime feedback for fast algorithms and queued/background execution for medium and slow analyses.
Legend and inspection UI so a user can understand what a visible band means for the selected algorithm.
success_criteria: A user can paste a 1,000-word passage and see realtime algorithm families render within one interaction cycle without blocking typing.
A user can enable several overlapping highlight families and the visible result consistently follows the documented lowest-band precedence rule.
The app can expose at least 20 distinct algorithm outputs in v1 across readability, style, structure, sentiment, lexical, rhythm, and repetition categories.
The UI clearly communicates whether an analysis is instant, loading, or deferred, and users can toggle each algorithm independently.
A new user can understand the banding model and control layout without reading external documentation.
non_goals: No user accounts, sync, or cloud document storage in v1.
No automated rewriting or generative text suggestions in v1.
No mobile-native app in v1; responsive web only.
target_users: Writers who want dense editorial feedback without reading long diagnostic reports.
Students and researchers comparing multiple ways to score the same passage.
Editors who want to isolate weak spans by linguistic or statistical signals.
style_preferences: Editorial, minimalist, monochrome control chrome with intentional use of color only inside the analysis system.
Sharp, data-tool feel rather than playful productivity app.
Avoid glossy gradients, skeuomorphic panels, or crowded dashboard aesthetics.
